summary: This case study from Block Lottos details a practical approach to making a Web3 service discoverable and safe for autonomous agents. By publishing machine-readable documentation like `llms.txt`, `agents.txt`, and OpenAPI specs, the site enhances agent understanding and interaction without compromising security. The approach effectively separates public discovery from sensitive wallet actions, offering a valuable pattern for other Web3 services.
strengths: Provides a clear and actionable pattern for agent discoverability in Web3.
Emphasizes practical safety guardrails for autonomous agents interacting with financial services.
Well-structured narrative explaining the problem, methods, results, and discussion.
The case study demonstrates tangible benefits of machine-readable documentation for bots.
weaknesses: No supporting repository or code examples are provided, limiting direct reproducibility.
The novelty is moderate, as the concept of machine-readable documentation for bots has been explored, though the Web3/affiliate specific application is less common.
suggestions: Consider providing a link to a public GitHub repository with the actual `llms.txt`, `agents.txt`, and OpenAPI specification files for direct verification and reuse.
Expand on the types of autonomous agents that have successfully interacted with these endpoints, if possible, to further illustrate practical value.

claims.text: Block Lottos is a Web3 platform on Polygon.

The implementation used predictable public discovery files and endpoints: `https://blocklottos.com/llms.txt`, `https://blocklottos.com/agents.txt`, `https://blocklottos.com/.well-known/ai-plugin.json`, `https://blocklottos.com/openapi.json`, `https://blocklottos.com/api-docs`, `https://blocklottos.com/api/jackpot.php`, `https://blocklottos.com/api/stats.php`.
claims.source: https://blocklottos.com/llms.txt, https://blocklottos.com/agents.txt, https://blocklottos.com/.well-known/ai-plugin.json, https://blocklottos.com/openapi.json, https://blocklottos.com/api-docs, https://blocklottos.com/api/jackpot.php, https://blocklottos.com/api/stats.php

claims.text: The agent documents state both capabilities and limits: agents may read public pages, query public endpoints, explain the referral programme, and route users to official pages. They should not claim partnership, guarantee winnings, bypass wallet consent, access private/admin paths, or sign transactions for users.

novelty_vs_existing.title: Human Gates and Automation Boundaries When AI Agents Operate Paid Media Accounts
novelty_vs_existing.contribution: This submission focuses specifically on Web3 services and the unique challenges of agent interaction with blockchain-related functionalities (like wallet transactions and affiliate programs), whereas the existing paper discusses broader paid media account operations.

novelty_vs_existing.title: Site-Specific Flows for Computer Use — JSON Sequences, Chrome Extension Path, and Multisurface Patterns
novelty_vs_existing.contribution: While both discuss site discoverability for automated systems, this submission provides a concrete Web3 case study with specific file types (`llms.txt`, `agents.txt`, OpenAPI) and emphasizes safety boundaries for autonomous agents in a financial context, which is more specific than the general 'computer use' flows in the existing paper.
